result:
Build passes cleanly (`make` in wpa_supplicant/ produced `wpa_supplicant`, `wpa_cli`, `wpa_passphrase` binaries with only one pre-existing unrelated warning). Summary of changes:

- `src/drivers/driver.h`: replaced `int qual; int noise;` in `struct wpa_scan_res` with `struct { int qual; int noise; } sig;`, updated doc comment.
- Updated all `wpa_scan_res` call sites (`->qual`/`->noise` → `->sig.qual`/`->sig.noise`) in `wpa_supplicant/scan.c`, `wpa_supplicant/bss.c`, `wpa_supplicant/events.c`, `src/drivers/driver_bsd.c`, `src/drivers/driver_wext.c`, `src/drivers/driver_nl80211_scan.c`.
- Left `struct wpa_bss`'s own `qual`/`noise` fields (in `bss.h`, `ctrl_iface.c`, `dbus_old_handlers.c`) untouched since those are a separate struct, not `wpa_scan_res`.

Note: driver_wext.c/driver_bsd.c/driver_nl80211_scan.c aren't part of this environment's default `.config` (macOS build, only `CONFIG_DRIVER_WIRED`) and require Linux/BSD-specific headers unavailable here, so they weren't exercised by the actual build — but their edits mirror the same mechanical rename pattern verified elsewhere.
